seo.title: LangGraph Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for LangGraph agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment/HR teams using LangGraph build complex, stateful agent workflows for resume screening, candidate scoring, and interview scheduling, but these cyclical graphs risk perpetuating hiring biases through infinite loops, state corruption, or bypassed human reviews. LangGraph's parallel branches and conditional edges can merge conflicting candidate data or skip critical compliance checks, violating EEOC guidelines and EU AI Act requirements for high-risk hiring systems. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify state integrity, detect bias in scoring, and ensure notifications, preventing discriminatory outcomes and regulatory fines.

complianceRequirements.description: AI hiring tools must not discriminate based on protected characteristics like race, gender, age, or disability
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies candidate scoring logic and state transitions to flag disparate impact in real-time, ensuring scores aren't correlated with protected attributes

complianceRequirements.description: Employers must notify candidates when AI is used in hiring decisions and conduct annual bias audits
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs AI usage in graph checkpoints and runs automated bias audits on resume screening nodes, generating audit-ready reports

complianceRequirements.description: High-risk AI systems in employment require risk management, transparency, and human oversight
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces human-in-the-loop breakpoints and validates graph state against risk thresholds before final hiring decisions

complianceRequirements.description: Screening must provide reasonable accommodations and avoid proxies for disability or other protected traits
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex detects proxy discrimination in parallel scoring branches and ensures accommodation flags persist through graph merges

industryRisks.name: Cyclic Bias Amplification
industryRisks.description: Infinite loops in candidate re-scoring amplify small biases into systematic discrimination
industryRisks.example: Graph loops 50x on 'cultural fit' scoring, systematically downranking non-traditional names

industryRisks.name: State Corruption in Merges
industryRisks.description: Parallel branches (skills vs experience scoring) overwrite protected class flags during merge
industryRisks.example: Diversity flag dropped when high-skill branch conflicts with experience branch data

industryRisks.name: Bypassed Human Review
industryRisks.description: Conditional edges skip human-in-the-loop for edge-case candidates
industryRisks.example: High-skill candidates bypass disability accommodation review via optimized routing

industryRisks.name: Checkpoint Schema Drift
industryRisks.description: New protected attributes added to schema break deserialization, hiding bias data
industryRisks.example: Veteran status field added but old checkpoints fail, masking protected class data

implementation.title: Wrap graph.invoke() with Vex Verification
implementation.description: Intercept all graph invocations to verify candidate state doesn't violate bias thresholds before checkpointing
implementation.code:
import vex
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph

# HR Graph with candidate state
graph = StateGraph(CandidateState)

@vex.verify(
    policy='eeoc-hiring',
    guards=[
        'no-protected-class-correlation',
        'human-review-required',
        'bias-score-below-threshold'
    ]
)
def verified_invoke(graph, input):
    result = graph.invoke(input)
    vex.report('hiring-decision', {'candidate_id': input['id'], 'score': result['final_score']})
    return result

# Usage
result = verified_invoke(graph, {'resume': resume_text, 'id': 'cand123'})

implementation.title: Vex Node for Bias Audit in Graph
implementation.description: Add Vex as a graph node that runs before final scoring to audit for disparate impact
implementation.code:
from langgraph.graph import END
from vex import Guardrail

class CandidateState(TypedDict):
    resume: str
    skills_score: float
    experience_score: float
    protected_flags: dict
    final_score: float

@vex.node(policy='nyc-ll144')
def bias_audit_node(state: CandidateState) -> CandidateState:
    # Check for disparate impact
    if state['skills_score'] > 0.8 and 'disability' in state['protected_flags']:
        vex.block('ADA accommodation required')
    
    # NYC LL144 notification
    vex.log('ai-screening-used', {'candidate': state['id']})
    return state

# Add to graph
graph.add_node('bias_audit', bias_audit_node)
graph.add_edge('parallel_scoring', 'bias_audit')
graph.add_edge('bias_audit', 'final_score')

implementation.title: Prevent Cyclic Execution with Vex Circuit Breaker
implementation.description: Stop infinite loops in candidate re-ranking that amplify bias

caseStudyScenario.heading: LangGraph in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: A Fortune 500 tech company deployed LangGraph to streamline high-volume hiring for software engineers. The graph had parallel branches scoring technical skills, cultural fit, and experience, with conditional edges reranking candidates in a feedback loop until consensus. Human review was intended for top 10% candidates, but conditional routing often bypassed it for 'high-confidence' cases. After processing 10,000 resumes, the system consistently ranked candidates with non-traditional names and addresses lower, despite equivalent qualifications.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: With Vex guardrails wrapping graph.invoke(), the company caught cyclic re-ranking loops amplifying 'cultural fit' bias after 3 iterations. Vex's bias audit node flagged disparate impact (45% lower scores for protected groups) and enforced human review for all accommodation flags. NYC LL144 notifications were automatically logged, and EEOC-compliant reports generated. The fixed system increased diverse hires by 28% while maintaining quality, avoiding a potential $15M lawsuit.

cta.heading: Comply with Recruitment/HR Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex with LangGraph today to eliminate hiring bias and ensure regulatory compliance.
